w:r.w:t: UI Design Reference: Aura Design System (Broadcast Retro Aesthetic)

Aura is a free, zero-cost, ad-free music streaming Android application that uses YouTube as its audio source backend. The app is built for users who are frustrated with Spotify's ad-heavy free tier and want a seamless, premium listening experience without a monthly subscription.

This document defines all product features, prioritised by phase, so that the Anti-Gravity development team can build the application in a structured, iterative manner. The UI design system (Broadcast Retro Aesthetic — warm paper tones, ink typography, FM radio motifs) is provided separately by the design team and is referenced throughout this document.
2. What Makes Spotify the World's #1 Music App

Before defining what Aura must build, it is critical to understand why Spotify dominates. With 675 million users and 260 million paid subscribers, Spotify's dominance comes from four interlocked pillars:
100 million+ songs and 5 million+ podcasts, available on demand
Zero friction from search to playback — under 3 seconds
Cross-device sync — phone, desktop, web, TV, car, smartwatch, gaming consoles
Discover Weekly — a 30-song playlist refreshed every Monday, personalised by listening history
Release Radar — new releases from followed artists, refreshed every Friday
Daily Mixes — 6 genre-based mixes generated from the user's taste profile
Spotify Radio — infinite station generated from any song, artist, or playlist
AI DJ — an AI voice that introduces curated music blocks like a radio presenter
Taste Profile — machine learning model tracking every play, skip, save, and search
Blend — a shared playlist that mixes two users' tastes into one collaborative feed
Create unlimited playlists with custom names, covers, and descriptions
Collaborative playlists — invite others to add tracks
Playlist folders — organise playlists into nested folders
Smart Shuffle — shuffles but interleaves recommended songs between your tracks
Queue management — add to queue, reorder, view upcoming tracks
Liked Songs — a special auto-playlist of all heart-saved tracks
Offline Backup — automatically caches recently played songs for dead zones
Pin playlists, albums, and artists to the top of Your Library
Gapless playback — no silence between tracks
Crossfade — smooth fade transition between songs (0–12 seconds, adjustable)
Equalizer — custom bass, treble, and frequency adjustment
Volume normalisation — consistent volume across all tracks
Streaming quality tiers — Low / Normal / High / Very High (320 kbps) / HiFi lossless
Sleep Timer — stop playback after a set time
Instant search by song, artist, album, playlist, podcast, genre, mood, or year
Filter search results by category (Songs, Artists, Albums, Playlists, Podcasts)
Browse by genre, mood, decade, and activity (workout, focus, sleep, party)
Charts — Global Top 50, Viral 50, regional charts
New Releases — curated new music by genre
Behind the Lyrics — song meaning and artist story cards
Follow friends and see what they are listening to (Friend Activity sidebar)
Share songs, albums, and playlists to WhatsApp, Instagram, X, etc.
Spotify Codes — scannable QR-style codes for instant sharing
Jam — shared listening session, friends control the same queue in real-time
In-app messaging — send songs and playlists to contacts inside Spotify
Spotify Wrapped — annual personalised stats (top songs, artists, minutes, genres)

Aura is essentially a premium Spotify-like UI shell that uses YouTube as a free, unlimited music library. Because YouTube hosts virtually every song ever released, Aura has a larger effective catalogue than Spotify — at zero cost to the user and zero cost to run.

The key technology behind Aura is the NewPipe Extractor library (open-source, no API key required), which resolves YouTube video URLs into audio-only streams. The Android app plays these streams via ExoPlayer/Media3, presenting a fully featured music player UI without any video or ads.
Audio streaming: YouTube audio via NewPipe Extractor → ExoPlayer / Media3
Search: YouTube Data API v3 (10,000 free queries/day) + YouTube Search via NewPipe
Local database: Room (SQLite) — stores playlists, liked songs, history, queue
No backend server required — the app is entirely self-contained on the device
No hosting costs, no AWS, no cloud storage
Distribution: APK sideload (not Play Store due to YouTube ToS grey area)

Note for developers: Aura operates in a legal grey area — it does not violate any law, but it does technically violate YouTube's Terms of Service by bypassing their player. This is the same approach used by open-source apps like ViMusic, InnerTune, and NewPipe, which are distributed as APKs on GitHub. Aura should not be submitted to the Google Play Store. It can be shared freely as an APK.

The MVP is the smallest version of Aura that the primary user can use daily as a full Spotify replacement. Based on stated usage patterns (open app → go to playlist → hit shuffle → listen), the MVP must nail exactly these three flows before anything else.
As a user, I want to open the app, tap my playlist, hit shuffle, and have my music start playing immediately — exactly like Spotify — without ads, without paying, and without needing my laptop.
User pastes a Spotify playlist URL; app fetches all song names and artists via the Spotify API and saves them locally.
Search any song or artist. Results stream from YouTube as audio-only via NewPipe Extractor.
View imported/created playlists. Tap to open and play.
Randomly reorder playlist queue. Persistent shuffle toggle in player.
Standard transport controls. Hardware media button support.
Music continues when app is backgrounded or screen is locked.
Media notification with controls on lock screen and notification shade.
Create a blank playlist, give it a name, add songs from search.
Long-press or tap menu on any song to add to an existing playlist.
Heart icon on any song. All liked songs appear in a special Liked Songs playlist.
Search by song title or artist name. Results listed with play button.
Full-screen player with album art (YouTube thumbnail), song title, artist, progress bar, controls.
Persistent mini player bar above the bottom nav when music is playing.
View the current playback queue, reorder or remove tracks.
Tap or drag the progress bar to seek within a track.
Shows Recently Played and user playlists. Retro Broadcast UI design system.
Grid/list of all playlists, liked songs, and history.
This is the single most important onboarding feature. Without it, users must manually add 150+ songs, which kills adoption instantly.
User opens Aura for the first time and taps 'Import from Spotify'.
User pastes a Spotify playlist URL (e.g. https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M).
App extracts the playlist ID from the URL.
App calls the Spotify Web API (GET /playlists/{id}/tracks) using a Spotify Client Credentials token (server-to-server, no user login required) to fetch all track names and artist names.
Each track (song name + artist) is saved to the Room database under a new local playlist.
When user plays a track, app searches YouTube for '[song name] [artist] official audio' and streams the top result.
Import progress bar shows how many of the 150 songs have been mapped. Import happens in background — user can start listening immediately.
Spotify API: Use Client Credentials flow (no OAuth, no user login to Spotify required)
Spotify App must be registered at developer.spotify.com (Client ID + Secret embedded in app)
Handle pagination: Spotify returns 100 tracks per request, auto-fetch all pages for playlists > 100 songs
Rate limit: Spotify API allows ~180 requests/minute — batch requests to stay within limits
Offline resilience: Save all imported track metadata to Room DB immediately so the playlist is usable offline
Duplicate detection: If a song already exists in the library, do not add it twice
Edge cases: Private playlists (show error), deleted songs (skip with note), regional tracks (fall back to alternate YouTube search)
Filter out: results that are live performances, covers, or remixes (unless no other match exists)
Cache match: once a Spotify track is matched to a YouTube video ID, store the mapping in Room DB permanently
Once the MVP is stable and the user is using it daily, these features elevate Aura from a basic player to a proper Spotify competitor.
Download YouTube audio stream to device storage for offline playback. No internet required once downloaded.
Weekly personalised playlist generated from listening history + YouTube Music trending data.
Shuffle mode that interleaves similar recommended tracks between playlist songs.
Smooth audio fade between tracks. Adjustable 0–10 seconds.
Zero-gap transition between tracks (pre-buffer next track).
5-band or 10-band EQ with presets (Bass Boost, Treble, Flat, etc.).
Stop playback after X minutes or at end of current song.
Fetch lyrics via LRCLib (free, open API) and display synced karaoke-style.
Nest playlists inside folders for organisation.
Sort by recently added, alphabetical, artist, duration.
Track and display all previously played songs.
View all songs and albums by an artist (sourced from YouTube).
Play a full album in order.
Auto-level volume across tracks to prevent jarring jumps.
Invite another Aura user to add songs to a shared playlist via shared link.
Import from Apple Music playlist URL using Apple MusicKit API.
Full Android Auto support for car playback.
These are the features that would make Aura genuinely surpass Spotify in innovation. Build these only after V2 is solid.
AI voice that introduces song blocks, explains choices, and transitions between genre moods — like a late-night radio DJ.
User types a text prompt (e.g. 'rainy evening jazz') and AI generates a 20-song playlist from YouTube.
Detect time of day, weather (via API), or manual mood input to auto-suggest a playlist style.
Annual listening stats: top songs, artists, genres, total minutes, listening streaks.
Two users' listening histories merged into one shared playlist.
See what songs friends are playing in real-time (opt-in privacy setting).
Source podcasts from YouTube or a free RSS podcast directory.
Shared playback session — all participants hear the same song in sync.
Short looping video background behind album art while song plays (sourced from YouTube clip).
If user has a high-bandwidth connection, prefer highest-quality YouTube audio stream (128kbps→256kbps).
Control Aura playback from another device on the same Wi-Fi (like Spotify Connect).
Also scan device storage and play local MP3/FLAC files alongside YouTube-streamed tracks.
Real-time listening stats: plays today, total hours, favourite genres this week.
Generate an infinite similar-songs station from any track using YouTube recommendations.
Toggle that pauses all listening history tracking temporarily.

All screens must implement the Broadcast Retro design system (warm paper/cream tones in light mode, near-black charcoal in dark mode, one vermillion red accent, Playfair Display italic + Tenor Sans + DM Mono typography, FM radio motifs, film grain overlay).
Primary landing screen. Retro broadcast homepage.
Wordmark, ON AIR clock, Marquee ticker, Featured track hero, Tracklist/Recent plays, Frequency ruler, Bottom nav
Find any song, artist, or album.
Search input, Filter chips (Songs/Artists/Albums), Results list, Recent searches
All user playlists, liked songs, history.
Playlist grid, Liked Songs shortcut, Import from Spotify button, Sort/filter
Playlist header, Track list, Shuffle button, Play all, Add songs button, Edit/delete options
Album art / YouTube thumbnail, Title, Artist, Progress bar, Seek, Play/Pause/Skip/Prev, Shuffle, Repeat, Like, Add to playlist, Queue
URL paste field, Import button, Progress bar, Success state, Error handling
Welcome screen, Import CTA, Permissions request (storage, notifications)
Modern Android standard. Full Jetpack support.
Declarative UI matching the design system. Smooth animations.
Industry standard. Used by YouTube itself. Handles streaming, gapless, background.
Open-source. No API key for audio stream URL resolution. Used by ViMusic.
10,000 free search queries/day. More than enough.
Client Credentials flow — no user login to Spotify needed. Free.
Playlists, liked songs, history, YouTube URL cache.
REST API calls to Spotify and YouTube APIs.
Async album art / YouTube thumbnail loading.
Free, open lyrics API with synced LRC format support.
Week 1–2: Project setup, Jetpack Compose scaffold, ExoPlayer integration, basic audio stream playback from hardcoded YouTube URL
Week 3: NewPipe Extractor integration, YouTube search → audio stream → play flow
Week 4: Room database, playlist creation, liked songs, queue management
Week 5: Spotify playlist import flow (API + YouTube mapping + Room storage)
Week 6: All screens built with Broadcast Retro design system (Home, Search, Library, Player, Playlist Detail)
Week 7: Background playback, lock screen controls, media notification
Week 8: QA, edge cases, APK build and sideload testing on real device
Offline download, crossfade, gapless, equaliser, lyrics, sleep timer
AI DJ, AI playlist generator, mood detection
Aura Wrapped, social feed, Jam sessions
HiFi audio, Aura Connect, podcast support
All UI implementation must follow the Broadcast Retro design language. The reference design files (React JSX prototype) are provided separately. Key specifications:
#C4371B — single vermillion red. Used only for: ON AIR dot, active track indicator, featured section marker, nav active state, HOT badge, progress arc.
Playfair Display Italic — wordmark, song titles, hero titles, ghost watermarks
Tenor Sans — all uppercase labels, navigation, artist names, section headers
DM Mono Light — clock, BPM, duration, frequency labels, search input
FM frequency ruler with needle, spinning vinyl disc, scrolling marquee ticker, ON AIR blinking pill, film grain scanline overlay, ghost track number watermark
iPhone 16 Pro titanium CSS frame — side buttons, Dynamic Island, dual-radius bezel

Dark mode is a first-class citizen, not an afterthought. Every colour in light mode has a matching dark-mode variable. The toggle is a physical pill switch (☀/☽) in the header. All transitions are 0.6s ease. The red accent glows with box-shadow in dark mode. The grain overlay uses screen blend mode in dark (vs multiply in light).
App cold start to audio playing: under 4 seconds
Playlist of 150 songs imported from Spotify: under 30 seconds
UI frame rate: 60fps minimum, 120fps on supported devices
If a YouTube stream fails mid-song, automatically retry with next-best search result
Cached YouTube video ID used for repeat plays (no repeated API calls)
App should not crash on network loss — graceful offline state
No user data sent to any external server — everything stored locally on device
Spotify API credentials stored using Android Keystore, not hardcoded in APK
No analytics, no tracking, no ads
Content descriptions on all interactive elements
Q1: Should the Spotify Client ID/Secret be embedded in the APK or fetched from a lightweight config endpoint? (Embedded is simpler but rotatable credentials require an update push.)
Q2: NewPipe Extractor occasionally breaks when YouTube changes its internal API. What is the maintenance/update strategy?
Q3: For the AI features in V3 (AI DJ, AI playlist), which LLM API will be used — Claude API, OpenAI, or on-device Gemini Nano?
Q4: Will there be user accounts, or is the app fully local-only? (Accounts enable cross-device sync but require a backend.)
Q5: What is the APK distribution strategy — GitHub Releases, a direct download page, or a custom F-Droid repository?
The single highest-leverage MVP feature is:
If a user can paste their Spotify playlist URL, wait 20 seconds, and then hit Shuffle Play — the app has delivered its core value proposition on day one.
Everything else is incremental. Build in this order:
YouTube audio stream playback working (ExoPlayer + NewPipe)
Spotify playlist import (paste URL → fetch tracks → Room DB)
Playlist view + Shuffle play (the daily-use flow)
Full UI with Broadcast Retro design system applied to all screens
Search, liked songs, queue, now playing screen
APK build, test on physical device, ship
